differentials.name: Ectopic hyperthyroidism =  extrathyroidal source of thyroid hormone (a)  Thyrotoxicosis factitia / medicamentosa = factitious surreptitious (stealthy) self-administration of thyroid hormones (eg, hamburger thyroiditis ← bovine thyroid in hamburger meat) (b)  Struma ovarii =  monodermal ovarian teratoma containing thyroid tissue
differentials.raw: Ectopic hyperthyroidism =  extrathyroidal source of thyroid hormone (a)  Thyrotoxicosis factitia / medicamentosa = factitious surreptitious (stealthy) self-administration of thyroid hormones (eg, hamburger thyroiditis ← bovine thyroid in hamburger meat) (b)  Struma ovarii =  monodermal ovarian teratoma containing thyroid tissue

differentials.name: PRIMARY HYPOTHYROIDISM (most common) =  thyroid's inability to produce sufficient thyroid hormone
differentials.raw: PRIMARY HYPOTHYROIDISM (most common) =  thyroid's inability to produce sufficient thyroid hormone

differentials.name: TSH >10 mU/L + FT4 below reference range
differentials.raw: TSH >10 mU/L + FT4 below reference range

differentials.name: SECONDARY HYPOTHYROIDISM =  failure of anterior pituitary to release sufficient quantities of TSH
differentials.raw: SECONDARY HYPOTHYROIDISM =  failure of anterior pituitary to release sufficient quantities of TSH

differentials.name: low to mildly elevated TSH + low FT4
differentials.raw: low to mildly elevated TSH + low FT4

differentials.name: TERTIARY / HYPOTHALAMIC HYPOTHYROIDISM =  failure of hypothalamus to produce sufficient amounts of TRH
differentials.raw: TERTIARY / HYPOTHALAMIC HYPOTHYROIDISM =  failure of hypothalamus to produce sufficient amounts of TRH

context: Frequency:   up to 3% of all teratomas (malignant teratoma in 24% of cases) Peak age:   50 years √  multilocular cystic ovarian mass with solid components MR: √  multiple intracystic areas with low SI on T2WI + intermediate SI on T1WI NUC: √  higher uptake than thyroid gland DDx:   thyroid cancer metastatic to the ovary Prognosis:   > 95% benign; malignant transformation to papillary / follicular thyroid cancer (rare) (c)  Metastatic functional thyroid cancer (very rare, 25 cases) Hypothyroidism
